name: Chin Tuck
instruction: Sit tall. Pull your chin straight back, making a double chin. Hold 5 seconds. Release. Repeat 5 times.
formCue: Keep eyes level — don't tilt your head down.

source.citation: Video: Neck stretches for the workplace

name: Lateral Neck Stretch
instruction: Tilt your right ear toward your right shoulder. Hold 15 seconds. Switch sides.
formCue: Drop the opposite shoulder down and away from your ear.

source.citation: Video: Neck stretches for the workplace

name: Levator Scapulae Release
instruction: Turn your head 45° to the right. Tuck your chin toward your collarbone. Hold 15 seconds. Switch sides.
formCue: You should feel the stretch along the back-side of your neck.

source.citation: Neck Pain: 6 Common Causes and Treatments

name: Neck Flexion Hold
instruction: Drop your chin slowly to your chest. Let gravity pull gently. Hold 15 seconds. Slowly return. Repeat once.
formCue: Relax your shoulders — don't hunch them up.

source.citation: Don't sit: Exercises for the office

name: Isometric Neck Press
instruction: Place your palm on your forehead. Press your head into your hand without moving. Hold 10 seconds. Repeat with hand on each side.
formCue: Resist evenly — your head shouldn't actually move.

source.citation: Physical Therapy Guide to Neck Pain

name: Scalene Stretch
instruction: Place your right hand behind your back. Tilt your left ear toward your left shoulder. Hold 15 seconds. Switch sides.
formCue: The arm behind your back anchors the shoulder — that deepens the stretch.

source.citation: Neck pain: Core exercises can help

name: Shoulder Shrug & Roll
instruction: Shrug both shoulders up to your ears. Hold 3 seconds. Roll them back and down. Repeat 5 times.
formCue: Exaggerate the backward roll — open your chest at the bottom.

source.citation: Desk stretches: Video collection

name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
instruction: Bring your right arm across your chest. Use your left hand to press it closer. Hold 15 seconds. Switch sides.
formCue: Keep the stretching arm straight — don't bend the elbow.

source.citation: Don't sit: Exercises for the office

name: Scapular Squeeze
instruction: Sit tall, arms at your sides. Squeeze your shoulder blades together as if pinching a pencil between them. Hold 5 seconds. Repeat 5 times.
formCue: Don't arch your lower back — the movement is all between the shoulder blades.

source.citation: 8 Posture Exercises To Sit and Stand Straighter

name: Overhead Reach & Side Lean
instruction: Interlace your fingers overhead, palms to ceiling. Lean gently to the right. Hold 10 seconds. Center. Lean left. Hold 10 seconds.
formCue: Keep both sit bones planted — the lean comes from your ribs, not your hips.

source.citation: Desk stretches: Video collection

name: Upper Trap Stretch
instruction: Sit on your right hand. Tilt your left ear toward your left shoulder. Hold 15 seconds. Switch sides.
formCue: Sitting on your hand anchors the shoulder down — you'll feel more stretch.

source.citation: Physical Therapy Guide to Neck Pain

name: Eagle Arms
instruction: Extend arms forward. Cross right arm over left at the elbows. Bend elbows and press palms together. Lift elbows slightly. Hold 15 seconds. Switch arm positions.
formCue: If palms don't touch, press the backs of your hands together instead.

source.citation: Stretching: 35 exercises to improve flexibility and reduce pain

name: Seated Chest Opener
instruction: Clasp your hands behind your back. Straighten your arms and gently lift them while opening your chest. Hold 15 seconds. Release. Repeat once.
formCue: Pull your shoulder blades down and together — don't shrug.

source.citation: 8 Posture Exercises To Sit and Stand Straighter

name: Seated Cat-Cow
instruction: Hands on knees. Exhale and round your back, chin to chest (cat). Inhale and arch your back, chest forward, look up (cow). Alternate 5 times.
formCue: Move slowly — one full breath per position.

source.citation: Back exercises in 15 minutes a day

name: Doorframe-Free Pec Stretch
instruction: Place both hands on the back of your head, elbows wide. Gently press your elbows back until you feel your chest open. Hold 15 seconds. Release. Repeat once.
formCue: Don't push your head forward — your hands rest lightly, elbows do the work.

source.citation: Exercise Posture Break for Office Workers

name: Thoracic Extension
instruction: Sit at the edge of your chair. Place hands behind your head. Gently arch your upper back over the chair back. Hold 10 seconds. Return. Repeat twice.
formCue: The bend should be in your upper back, not your lower back.

source.citation: Cat-Cow Stretch: A Gentle Move With Big Benefits

name: Seated Arm Circles
instruction: Extend both arms to the sides at shoulder height. Make 10 small forward circles, then 10 backward. Lower arms.
formCue: Keep circles small and controlled — shoulder blades should glide, not shrug.

source.citation: Don't sit: Exercises for the office

name: W-Arm Wall-Free Pull
instruction: Raise arms to goal-post position (elbows at 90°, upper arms at shoulder height). Squeeze shoulder blades and pull elbows down and back, making a W shape. Hold 5 seconds. Repeat 5 times.
formCue: Think "put your shoulder blades in your back pockets."

source.citation: Is it too late to save your posture?

name: Wrist Flexor Stretch
instruction: Extend your right arm, palm up. With your left hand, gently pull your fingers down and back. Hold 15 seconds. Switch hands.
formCue: Keep the stretching arm straight — elbow locked.

source.citation: 3 Stretches To Prevent or Relieve Carpal Tunnel

name: Wrist Extensor Stretch
instruction: Extend your right arm, palm down. With your left hand, gently press the back of your hand down. Hold 15 seconds. Switch hands.
formCue: You should feel the stretch along the top of your forearm.

source.citation: Video: Wrist and forearm stretches for the workplace

name: Fist-to-Fan
instruction: Make tight fists with both hands. Hold 3 seconds. Spread all fingers wide. Hold 3 seconds. Repeat 5 times.
formCue: Spread fingers as far apart as possible on the open phase.

source.citation: 11 Exercises and Stretches for Wrist Pain

name: Wrist Circles
instruction: Extend both arms in front of you. Make slow circles with your wrists — 10 clockwise, then 10 counterclockwise.
formCue: Keep your forearms still — only your hands move.

source.citation: Video: Wrist and forearm stretches for the workplace

name: Prayer Stretch
instruction: Press palms together in front of your chest, fingers pointing up. Slowly lower your hands toward your waist, keeping palms pressed. Hold where you feel the stretch for 15 seconds.
formCue: Keep your elbows lifting outward as your hands lower.

source.citation: 3 Stretches To Prevent or Relieve Carpal Tunnel

name: Thumb Flexion Stretch
instruction: Extend your right hand open. Use your left hand to gently pull your right thumb down and across your palm. Hold 15 seconds. Switch hands.
formCue: Stop if you feel sharp pain — this should be a mild pull only.

source.citation: 11 Exercises and Stretches for Wrist Pain

name: Seated Spinal Twist
instruction: Sit tall. Place your right hand on your left knee. Twist gently to the left, looking over your left shoulder. Hold 15 seconds. Switch sides.
formCue: Sit up tall first, then twist — don't slouch into the rotation.

source.citation: A plan for easy stretching

name: Seated Figure-Four
instruction: Cross your right ankle over your left knee. Sit tall and gently lean forward from your hips. Hold 15 seconds. Switch legs.
formCue: Keep your back straight — the lean is from the hips, not the shoulders.

source.citation: Physical Therapy Guide to Hip Impingement (Femoroacetabular Impingement)

name: Seated Knee-to-Chest
instruction: Grab behind your right thigh and pull your knee toward your chest. Hold 15 seconds. Switch legs.
formCue: Keep your back straight — don't round forward to meet your knee.

source.citation: Don't sit: Exercises for the office

name: Pelvic Tilt
instruction: Sit tall. Tilt your pelvis forward, arching your low back slightly. Hold 5 seconds. Tilt backward, flattening your low back. Hold 5 seconds. Repeat 3 times.
formCue: The movement is small and low — your shoulders shouldn't move much.

source.citation: These Lower Back Stretches and Exercises Can Help Relieve Pain

name: Seated Hip Flexor Stretch
instruction: Scoot to the right edge of your chair. Let your left leg slide back, foot on floor behind you. Press your hips slightly forward. Hold 15 seconds. Switch sides.
formCue: Keep your torso upright — don't lean forward.

source.citation: 30-Minute Home Stretching Program

name: Seated Glute Stretch
instruction: Cross your right ankle over your left knee. Place your right hand on your right knee and gently press down. Hold 15 seconds. Switch sides.
formCue: Sit tall throughout — pressing the knee works better when your spine is straight.

source.citation: Stretching: 35 exercises to improve flexibility and reduce pain

name: Seated Hamstring Stretch
instruction: Extend your right leg straight, heel on the floor. Sit tall and hinge forward from your hips until you feel a stretch behind your thigh. Hold 15 seconds. Switch legs.
formCue: Keep your back flat — hinge at the hips, don't round your spine.

source.citation: Hamstring stretch

name: Ankle Circles
instruction: Lift your right foot off the floor. Draw slow circles with your toes — 10 clockwise, then 10 counterclockwise. Switch feet.
formCue: Make the circles as large as your ankle allows — full range of motion.

source.citation: How To Strengthen Your Ankles

name: Calf Raise (Seated)
instruction: Place both feet flat on the floor. Lift your heels as high as possible, pressing onto the balls of your feet. Hold 3 seconds. Lower. Repeat 8 times.
formCue: Push evenly through all five toes — don't roll to the outside edge.

source.citation: 30-Minute Home Strengthening Program

name: Toe Flexion & Extension
instruction: With feet flat on the floor, curl your toes tightly. Hold 5 seconds. Lift and spread all toes wide. Hold 5 seconds. Repeat 3 times.
formCue: Keep your feet planted — only your toes should move.

source.citation: 4 Best Exercises for Flat Feet and Fallen Arches

name: Seated Quad Stretch
instruction: Scoot to the edge of your chair. Bend your right knee and reach back to grab your right ankle. Gently pull your heel toward your glute. Hold 15 seconds. Switch legs.
formCue: Keep your knees close together — don't let the stretching knee flare out.

source.citation: Don't sit: Exercises for the office

name: Ankle Dorsiflexion Stretch
instruction: Place your right foot flat on the floor. Keeping your heel down, slide your knee forward over your toes as far as comfortable. Hold 15 seconds. Switch feet.
formCue: Heel stays glued to the floor — the stretch is in the back of your ankle.

source.citation: Exercises and stretches to keep your feet healthy
